mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals improving labor market conditions, which boosts corporate earnings visibility and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5% despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 100 signals strengthening manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive equity risk premiums and drives S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 60-day returns in 50%+ of cases where INDPRO exceeds 100 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above its 3-month moving average indicates sustained economic growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 40%+ of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal central bank responsiveness to inflation, which creates uncertainty and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS exceed their thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in initial jobless claims (PAYEMS) during high unemployment (UNRATE > 7%) signals labor market stabilization, which reduces recession risks and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 60-day returns in 50%+ of cases where PAYEMS declines and UNRATE exceeds 7%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.2 million units signals strong consumer confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 50%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 1.2M.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real savings (RSAFS) below its 12-month low signals reduced consumer liquidity, which increases equity market volatility and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20-day returns in 55%+ of cases where RSAFS falls below 500B.
